alias_policy: temporary aliases warn on stderr, forward to the canonical handler, share the same state root, create no duplicate controller, preserve scientific identity

entries.affected_automation: launch scripts, HIDE desktop hide-serve; none broken.
entries.compatibility_alias: `serve` kept as the canonical verb (no rename).
entries.warning_text: none

entries.affected_automation: batched-capture/serve-report harnesses, eval scripts calling generate --prompts-file.
entries.warning_text: `hawking generate` is now `hawking serve <artifact> --once`; forwarding. Alias removed after 2 releases.
`tokenize` is now `doctor --tokenize` (read-only parity diagnostic).

entries.affected_automation: tools/bench/* wrappers, paired_lever.sh, dead_lever registry runs.
entries.warning_text: `bench` is now `doctor --bench` (read-only measurement).

`bench-q4k-shapes` folded into `doctor --bench q4k-shapes`.

entries.compatibility_alias: `doctor` kept (canonical), semantics widened to model-OR-artifact + read-only default.
entries.warning_text: none (behavior superset; QAT/KD 'heal' now under --treat).

`batch-hash` folded into `doctor --verify` (per-prompt b3sum determinism check).
`shader-hash` folded into `status --version` build identity.
`verify` is now `doctor --verify` (SHA + sidecar + ledger + parity).
`bake-sidecar` folded into `condense`; sidecar is a condense output.
`press` folded into `condense`; `press --dry-run` == `condense --explain`.

entries.new_five_command_equivalent: retained verbatim as a global flag on all 5 commands
entries.warning_text: none

entries.compatibility_alias: seed-c bin retained as internal engine; `hawking gravity` is the front door.
entries.removal_condition: keep bin internal, unpublish as user surface
entries.warning_text: `hawking-seed-c run` is now the engine behind `hawking gravity`; call gravity.

entries.compatibility_alias: internal stage; f2 divergence measurement -> doctor --verify.
entries.new_five_command_equivalent: gravity (F2 forge stage) / doctor --verify (F2 divergence fixture)
entries.warning_text: `f2` is an internal gravity stage; parity view via `doctor --verify`.
`gravity-run` is now `hawking gravity`.
`seed-c status` folded into `hawking status`.
`inspect` folded into `status --explain` / `doctor`.
`verify` folded into `doctor --verify`.
`drain` is now `gravity --drain` (graceful, lease-releasing).
`resume` is now `gravity --resume`.

entries.affected_automation: launchd KeepAlive stop, kill scripts -> must call --drain.
entries.compatibility_alias: no hard-kill verb exposed; drain is the only stop.
entries.warning_text: There is no `stop`; use `--drain` (graceful, seals state). Hard-kill is not a public action.

entries.affected_automation: THE LIVE RUN (PID 84690 lease), launchd autoresume, resume hooks.
entries.compatibility_alias: SCRIPT PRESERVED AS-IS; `gravity` shells out to it. NOT rewritten, NOT broken.
entries.removal_condition: NEVER remove while a live campaign can use it; only unpublish as the recommended surface after the run completes
entries.warning_text: `second_light_controller.py` is the live controller behind `gravity`; keep invoking it directly is supported during migration.

entries.compatibility_alias: SCRIPT PRESERVED AS-IS; `status` shells out to / re-implements its exact read-only-flock contract.
entries.removal_condition: NEVER break; it and its import (second_light_controller) must keep working.
entries.warning_text: `second_light_status.py` is the live status truth behind `hawking status`; unchanged.

entries.affected_automation: one-run ignition; refused anyway by singleton lease.
entries.warning_text: Ignition folded into `gravity`; `--dry-run` -> `gravity --explain`.

entries.compatibility_alias: program/manifest generators stay internal to gravity; readiness/gates/evidence -> doctor --verify sections.
entries.removal_condition: unpublish as user surface; keep as gravity build steps
entries.warning_text: Do not run program/source_manifest/ignite mid-campaign (they reseal the bound program); use `gravity`/`doctor --verify`.
successor `gravity-*` read verbs folded into `hawking status`.
successor gravity write/validate verbs folded into `gravity` and `doctor --verify`.

entries.new_five_command_equivalent: status (read the queue) / gravity works it
entries.warning_text: `queue` view folded into `status`; the run works the queue, there is no separate queue command.

entries.compatibility_alias: -> gravity --explain and condense --explain; admission becomes automatic.

entries.warning_text: Frontier planning is now `gravity --explain`; press-plan is `condense --explain`; admission is automatic (no command).

entries.compatibility_alias: no command; harvesting is automatic on row-seal, surfaced in status.
entries.new_five_command_equivalent: gravity automatic behavior; status lists harvested artifacts
entries.warning_text: `harvest` is now automatic; completed artifacts appear in `status` / `doctor`.

entries.compatibility_alias: no command; retirement automatic. Plan view -> doctor --explain.
entries.new_five_command_equivalent: automatic GC on drain/complete; doctor --explain shows retire plan
entries.warning_text: `retire` is automatic; there is no retire command.
`eta` folded into `status`.

entries.compatibility_alias: calibration is an automatic gravity step; verify via doctor.
entries.warning_text: `calibrate` is an automatic gravity step; inspect via `doctor --verify`.

entries.affected_automation: live watchers; launchd plist installers must move out of the CLI.
entries.removal_condition: remove watch-plist immediately from public surface
entries.warning_text: `watch` is `status --watch`. `watch-plist` (launchd install) is removed: install persistence out-of-band, not a hawking command.
Scheduling/persistence arming is not a hawking command; the five commands are single-machine and stateless-invocation.

entries.affected_automation: Telegram rung notifiers, --notify flags on run scripts.
entries.compatibility_alias: none; notifications become an innate opt-in behavior read from config.
entries.removal_condition: remove command surface (keep notifier as internal library)
entries.warning_text: `telegram` is not a command; notifications are an automatic config-driven behavior.
`admission` is automatic inside `gravity`; no command.
eco Press->Summon pipeline folded into `condense`; passport/status into `doctor`.

entries.new_five_command_equivalent: gravity/condense internal (packing is a production step)
entries.removal_condition: keep as library; never a public command
entries.warning_text: Packing has no public command; it is internal to gravity/condense.
Hawking is single-machine; there are no worker/distributed commands. One heavy job at a time under the singleton lease.
Artifact listing is a `status` section; inspect one with `doctor`.

entries.compatibility_alias: -> doctor --verify; rebuild stays internal to gravity.
entries.warning_text: Source provenance is verified via `doctor --verify`; the manifest is a gravity internal.
All benchmarking is `doctor --bench` (read-only measurement).
All diagnostics live under `doctor`.

entries.new_five_command_equivalent: doctor <artifact> --treat  (heal in place) / condense --recover (heal while producing)
entries.warning_text: `treat` is `doctor --treat` (authorized, mutating) or `condense --recover`.
Event history is `status --history` (read-only tail of the hash-chained log).
Resource telemetry is a `status` section.
Artifact passport/provenance is a `doctor` section.

entries.affected_automation: forge tournaments, subbit packer experiments (share gravity_forge lib the campaign imports).
entries.compatibility_alias: -> gravity; standalone experiment scripts kept for research, unpublished as user surface.
entries.removal_condition: unpublish as user commands; keep libraries
entries.warning_text: The Forge is `hawking gravity`; forge_* remain internal research libraries.
Event Horizon is the engine behind `gravity`.

entries.compatibility_alias: kept as internal/research scripts; not part of the 5-command public surface.
entries.warning_text: The successor CLI and standalone runners are internal; use the 5 commands.

migration_must_alias_not_break.controller-bound seal generators: second_light_program.py / second_light_source_manifest.py / second_light_ignite.py reseal the program/manifest/lease the controller is bound to. Their aliases (`gravity`, `gravity --explain`) MUST refuse or route through the lease so they cannot re-seal or re-ignite mid-campaign; direct invocation stays possible but is guarded by the flock.
migration_must_alias_not_break.gravity: `hawking gravity <model>` and its --resume/--drain MUST shell out to / wrap the existing second_light_controller.py through the SAME singleton lease. It must NOT reimplement the controller in a way that could start a second controller on the same root. `reset` semantics map to `gravity --drain` then re-ignite -- never a hard kill of a live campaign.
migration_must_alias_not_break.status: `hawking status` and bare `hawking` MUST preserve second_light_status.py's exact contract: read-only, never acquire-and-hold the lease, liveness from a non-blocking flock probe, progress recounted from durable checkpoints. Breaking second_light_status.py OR its module-level import of second_light_controller breaks status; the migration keeps both importable and intact.

runtime_critical_do_not_break: tools/condense/second_light_controller.py (the durable Gravity controller; SecondLightController.run/resume; holds the lease) -- the engine behind `hawking gravity`.
tools/condense/second_light_status.py (imports second_light_controller at module level; STATUS_SCHEMA hawking.second_light.status.v1) -- the engine behind `hawking status`.

Their import closure: gravity_forge.py, second_light_pack.py (full-scope 128-expert path used by --max-experts 128), gptoss_moe_runtime.py, doctor_v5_gptoss_mxfp4.py, succ_watchdog.py (SingletonLease/heartbeat/sample_resources), succ_events.py (EventLog), and the no-__main__ libraries eco_common.py + studio_manifest.py.
safety_guarantees_encoded_in_the_design: One heavy job at a time: the singleton fcntl lease remains the single arbiter; `gravity` (any spelling/alias) that could drive the campaign routes through it, so no five-command rename can spawn a duplicate controller (INV-7).
status can never become a shadow controller (INV-6): no --resume/--drain on status, no lease-hold, no signals.
--drain is the only stop verb: graceful, seals resumable state, releases the lease; there is no public hard-kill (source stays integrity-frozen and resumable).

The five-command aliases are additive shims during migration -- the live scripts keep working unchanged, and the DO-NOT-BREAK import closure is never rewritten as part of this consolidation, only fronted.
live_run_safety.status_at_design_time: The audit captured second_light_controller.py live as PID 84690 holding the fcntl singleton lease. At the moment this design was written, ps/pgrep show that specific PID is NOT currently resident. This does NOT relax the constraint: the campaign is crash-resumable and launchd-armable, so a live controller may reappear at any time on the same campaign root, and the migration must be safe whether or not one is running right now.
